_note: Genuine comment starters. ALWAYS adapt to the specific reel before posting - identical comments pasted everywhere read as spam. No links in comments.

general: The animation in donghua got so good so fast, people sleeping on this genre are gonna regret it.
This is exactly the scene I show people when they say Chinese animation looks cheap.
Donghua really mastered the slow-burn payoff in a way most anime rushes past.
Wild how a 'side character' in donghua gets more development than some anime leads.
The soundtrack work in these is underrated, half the impact of this scene is the score.
a_mortals_journey: Han Li is the only protagonist who survives by assuming everyone wants him dead, and he's always right.
No bloodline, no cheat, just paranoia and patience. Han Li is built different.
People call Fanren slow but that patience is the entire point of the character.
Han Li would rather run and live than win and die. Smartest MC in cultivation imo.
battle_through_the_heavens: Xiao Yan eating flames that should eat him will never not be peak.
BTTH has been running forever and the action still goes insane, respect.
The Pure Lotus arc is where BTTH stops being revenge and becomes something bigger.
Say what you want, Xiao Yan's glow-up from clan reject to monster is still the blueprint.
perfect_world: Perfect World looks like a power fantasy until you realize it's actually about grief.
Shi Hao loses something every single arc and that's why it hits so hard.
The recent Perfect World arcs are straight up cinematic, every fight looks like a movie.
Hundreds of episodes and it still earns every emotional beat. Underrated long-runner.
renegade_immortal: Renegade Immortal is the darkest cultivation story animated and nobody warns you.
Wang Lin isn't a hero, he's a survivor who'll cross any line. That's the whole appeal.
Xian Ni never lets you forget the cost of every choice Wang Lin makes. Brutal and brilliant.
Tragic hero or monster the cultivation world created? With Wang Lin I genuinely can't decide.
mo_dao_zu_shi: MDZS proved cultivation stories could be about more than just getting stronger.
The whole point of MDZS is that the 'righteous' crowd was the real villain.
Wei Wuxian being hated for being right is peak tragedy writing.
Tencent clearly spent the money on this one, the animation still holds up.
shrouding_the_heavens: A bronze coffin in space as the opening is such an insane hook for a cultivation story.
Ye Fan deciding the universe's rules are negotiable is the most xianxia thing ever.
Shrouding the Heavens is the donghua for people who think they've seen everything.
The mystery-box worldbuilding in Zhe Tian is unmatched, ancient stars being tombs is wild.
power_scaling: Straight duel one of them wins, battle to the death the other does. Context changes everything.
Power scaling these is impossible because half of them just refuse to die on principle.
Hot take but feats mean nothing if the character is too dumb to use them. Brains > raw power.
Everyone scales by who hits hardest, I scale by who's still standing at the end.
beginner: If someone's new, start them on something short first or they'll drown and quit.
Honestly the biggest beginner trap is judging donghua by the first two episodes.
Best advice for newcomers: watch the official release, not the potato-quality reuploads.
Don't expect anime pacing, donghua tells it slower but the payoff is deeper.
